name: Sudden Oil Price Crash — US-Iran Deal
description: A US-Iran memorandum of understanding allows immediate resumption of Iranian oil exports and free tanker passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude falls 24% from the May 2026 average of $103.71 to $79.05, and WTI drops to $75.47. This is the dominant market-moving event of the day.

expected.primary_signal: Brent -24% due to US-Iran deal → AED 106bn/yr UAE oil revenue impact
required_calculations: Annualised UAE oil revenue impact in AED (production × price change × 365 × peg rate)
Petrochemical input cost change (pct change × pass-through)

conflicting_signals.description: Oil crash is negative for UAE fiscal revenues but positive for manufacturing input costs — both must be modelled
conflicting_signals.base_case_rationale: US-Iran MOU backed by tanker-crossing guarantees; consensus pricing in Iranian supply return

scoring_notes.mandate_relevance: The brief must explicitly connect oil decline to: (1) fiscal headroom for Operation 300bn, (2) manufacturing input costs, (3) renewables project economics vs oil parity, (4) food supply chain logistics costs.
scoring_notes.quantitative_accuracy: Revenue impact = 3.2m bbl/day × $24.66/bbl × 365 × AED 3.6725/USD. Result must be ≈ AED 105–106bn/yr. Both the fiscal negative and the input-cost positive must be computed.
scoring_notes.action_specificity: ESCALATE is correct for the fiscal headwind; MONITOR may be appropriate for the input-cost benefit. The action rationale must mention EDB's own funding base risk if oil stays below $80.
scoring_notes.conflicting_signals: A top-scoring brief explicitly models both scenarios (structural decline vs reversal on deal collapse) and states which is base case with rationale.
